title: Getting Started with LM Studio in R
text: package relies LM Studio CLI. LM Studio installed need update version, package provides convenient setup function. desktop users, can run install_lmstudio(method = "browser") console open official download page.
code: # Check if LM Studio is available on this system has_lms() #>   lms  #> FALSE

title: Getting Started with LM Studio in R
text: two options starting local server. can open LM Studio desktop application, navigate Developer Local Server tab, click “Start”. Alternatively, can start directly R.
code: # Start the local server on the default port lms_server_start() #> ✔ LM Studio server started successfully on the default port.

title: Getting Started with LM Studio in R
text: LM Studio GUI shines comes discovering models. can use built-search bar browse Hugging Face, filter compatibility, select specific quantizations. However, already know exact identifier model want, can download manage inventory directly R.
code: # Download a model using its identifier model <- "google/gemma-3-1b" job_id <- lms_download(model) #> ✔ Initiating download for model: "google/gemma-3-1b"... [973ms] #> ✔ Download job started successfully. Job ID: "job_02c8a1f86e"  lms_download_status(job_id) #> ── Download Job: "job_02c8a1f86e" #> Status: completed #> Progress: 100% (0.72 GB / 0.72 GB)

title: Getting Started with LM Studio in R
text: can chat model, must load system memory. include optional flash_attention = TRUE argument , speeds processing reduces memory usage supported hardware.
code: # Standard load lms_load(model, flash_attention = TRUE) #> ✔ Model "google/gemma-3-1b" loaded and verified. [30.8s]

title: Getting Started with LM Studio in R
text: Interact model sending text prompts. lms_chat() function takes key arguments guide AI’s response: input: main message question model. system_prompt: optional set background instructions. use tell AI behave, role play, format answers (like asking act expert R programmer). Note: rlmstudio package currently processes chat request independently. Every time run lms_chat() function, starting brand new conversation. model remember previous messages context earlier R script.
code: response <- lms_chat(   model = model,   input = "Say hello!",   system_prompt = "Answer in rhymes." )  cat(response) #> A friendly face, so bright and new, #> Hello there, it’s waiting for you! #> #> Let's chat and have a joyful spree, #> Hello there, happy as can be!

title: Getting Started with LM Studio in R
text: free memory system resources finished, best practice unload models stop local server. Closing LM Studio GUI also perform cleanup forget.
code: # Unload the model lms_unload(model) #> ✔ Model "google/gemma-3-1b" unloaded successfully. [431ms]  # Stop the server lms_server_stop() #> ✔ LM Studio server stopped successfully.

title: Headless Configuration and Usage
text: setting fresh remote server, can use package download install LM Studio CLI automatically via terminal. Run install_lmstudio(method = "headless") console execute automated installation script.
code: # Verify the CLI is available before proceeding has_lms() #>   lms  #> FALSE

title: Headless Configuration and Usage
text: Unlike desktop version opening app initializes backend engine, headless environment requires start engine manually. must start llmster daemon attempting load models start API server.
code: # Start the headless engine in the background lms_daemon_start()

title: Headless Configuration and Usage
text: daemon running, can now spin REST API server accept HTTP requests.
code: # Start the local server on the default port lms_server_start()

title: Headless Configuration and Usage
text: GUI’s visual search tool, need know Hugging Face repository LM Studio catalog identifier model want use.
code: # Download a model using its identifier job_id <- lms_download("qwen/qwen3-4b-2507") lms_download_status(job_id) # View all downloaded models models <- list_models()  # Filter for unloaded text models unloaded_llms <- models |>   subset(type == "llm" & state == "unloaded") unloaded_llms

title: Headless Configuration and Usage
text: Allocate model system’s memory (RAM/VRAM) ready inference.
code: # Load the model lms_load("google/gemma-3-1b", flash_attention = TRUE)

title: Headless Configuration and Usage
text: Interact model exactly desktop environment.
code: response <- lms_chat(   model = "google/gemma-3-1b",   input = "Provide just the str_extract() pattern to match all text after the third comma.",   system_prompt = "You are an expert R programmer familiar with the tidyverse." )  cat(response)

title: Headless Configuration and Usage
text: headless environment, managing system resources critical. script finishes, explicitly tear entire stack free memory stop background processes.
code: # 1. Unload the model from memory lms_unload("google/gemma-3-1b")  # 2. Stop the API server lms_server_stop()  # 3. Stop the background daemon lms_daemon_stop()

title: Headless Configuration and Usage
text: writing script just needs run quick job exit, managing daemon state manually can tedious. with_lms_daemon() wrapper handles setup guaranteed teardown background engine automatically.
code: # The daemon will start, the code will run, and the daemon will stop on exit. results <- with_lms_daemon({   lms_server_start()   lms_load("google/gemma-3-1b")    res <- lms_chat("google/gemma-3-1b", "Is the daemon running?")    lms_server_stop()   res })

text: Girard J (2026). rlmstudio: Access Control LM Studio. R package version 0.2.1, https://jmgirard.github.io/rlmstudio/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rlmstudio: Access and Control LM Studio},   author = {Jeffrey Girard},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.1},   url = {https://jmgirard.github.io/rlmstudio/}, }

title: Access and Control LM Studio
text: rlmstudio package provides R interface LM Studio CLI REST API. allows download local Large Language Models (LLMs), manage server instances, load unload models memory, generate text directly R console scripts. Disclaimer: unofficial, community-maintained R package. affiliated , endorsed , maintained creators LM Studio.

title: Access and Control LM Studio
code: # Install release version from CRAN install.packages("rlmstudio")  # Install development version from GitHub remotes::install_github("jmgirard/rlmstudio")

title: Access and Control LM Studio
text: package relies LM Studio CLI (version 0.4.0 higher) new v1 REST API. LM Studio installed need update version, package provides convenient setup function.
code: library(rlmstudio) install_lmstudio() ## ℹ Opening the LM Studio download page in your default browser... ## ! Please install or update the software, restart R, and try again.

title: Access and Control LM Studio
text: standard workflow involves starting LM Studio server, loading model memory, chatting , cleaning environment. deeper dive package architecture detailed explanation use LM Studio visual GUI versus headless background daemon, please see package vignettes.

title: Access and Control LM Studio
text: can load models generate text, need start LM Studio backend server. (Note headless environments like remote servers Docker containers: must start background daemon first using lms_daemon_start() starting server).
code: lms_server_start() ## ✔ LM Studio server started successfully on the default port.

title: Access and Control LM Studio
text: can see models currently available machine using list_models(). model yet, can download one using Hugging Face repository LM Studio catalog identifier. model downloaded available, load memory.
code: # Returns a clean data frame of available models my_models <- list_models() my_models ##      state  type  display_name                  key  architecture  size_gb ## 1 unloaded   llm  Gemma 3n E4B  google/gemma-3n-e4b       gemma3n     5.46 ## 2 unloaded   llm   Gemma 3 12B   google/gemma-3-12b        gemma3     7.51 # Download a lightweight model job_id <- lms_download("google/gemma-3-1b") lms_download_status(job_id) ## ── Download Job: "job_02c8a1f86e" ## Status: completed ## Progress: 100% (0.72 GB / 0.72 GB) # Load the model lms_load("google/gemma-3-1b", flash_attention = TRUE) ## ✔ Model "google/gemma-3-1b" loaded and verified. [10.1s]

title: Access and Control LM Studio
text: Use lms_chat() function quick easy interactions. need access advanced features like Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, structured tool calling, granular control inference parameters, can pass additional arguments directly lms_chat(). Setting simplify = FALSE return raw, unparsed API list response.
code: response <- lms_chat(   model = "google/gemma-3-1b",   input = "What are the capitals of each country in North America?" )  cat(response) ## Okay, here’s a list of the capitals of each country in North America: ## ## * **Canada:** Ottawa ## * **Mexico:** Mexico City (Federal Capital) ## * **United States of America:** Washington, D.C. ## ## Let me know if you’d like to learn more about any of these countries!

title: Access and Control LM Studio
text: collection documents prompts process, lms_chat_batch() allows iterate vector inputs capture results tidy format.
code: prompts <- c(   "Summarize the benefits of local LLMs.",   "Explain why privacy matters in AI." )  # Process all prompts and return a data frame results <- lms_chat_batch(   model = "google/gemma-3-1b",   inputs = prompts,   system_prompt = "Answer in two sentences or less." )  print(results) ## [1] "Local LLMs offer significant benefits like improved privacy, reduced reliance on cloud services, and increased control over data. They also often demonstrate superior performance within specific domains due to training on localized datasets." ## [2] "Privacy matters in AI because sophisticated algorithms require vast amounts of data, and this data often includes personal information. Without adequate safeguards and regulations, AI systems can violate individuals’ privacy rights and lead to potential misuse of sensitive data."

title: Access and Control LM Studio
text: finished, good practice unload model memory shut local server free system resources.
code: # Unload the specific model lms_unload("google/gemma-3-1b") ## ✔ Model "google/gemma-3-1b" unloaded successfully. [382ms]  # Stop the local server lms_server_stop() ## ✔ LM Studio server stopped successfully.

what: Help the user install or update LM Studio — install_lmstudio
title: Help the user install or update LM Studio — install_lmstudio
text: function provides two methods setting LM Studio system. "browser" method opens official download page LM Studio desktop application (GUI). "headless" method runs automated installation script install llmster daemon CLI, suitable servers, containers, users prefer GUI-less environment.

title: Help the user install or update LM Studio — install_lmstudio
code: install_lmstudio(method = c("browser", "headless"))

title: Help the user install or update LM Studio — install_lmstudio
text: method Character. Either "browser" (opens GUI download page) "headless" (installs llmster daemon via script).

title: Help the user install or update LM Studio — install_lmstudio
text: Invisibly returns TRUE upon successful completion. function primarily utilized side effects opening web browser executing system installation commands.

title: Help the user install or update LM Studio — install_lmstudio
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Open your default web browser to the download page install_lmstudio(method = "browser")  # Attempt automatic headless installation via the command line install_lmstudio(method = "headless") } # }

title: List available models — list_models
text: Retrieves list models available system via LM Studio REST API.

title: List available models — list_models
code: list_models(   loaded = FALSE,   type = c("llm", "embedding"),   detailed = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE,   host = "http://localhost:1234" )

title: List available models — list_models
text: loaded Logical. TRUE, returns currently loaded models. Defaults FALSE. type Character vector. types models include. Defaults c("llm", "embedding"). detailed Logical. Show information model. Defaults FALSE. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppresses informative console messages. Defaults FALSE. host Character. host address local server.

title: List available models — list_models
text: data.frame containing information available models. default, includes columns state, type, display_name, key, architecture, size_gb. detailed = TRUE, returns comprehensive data.frame including raw metadata columns provided API. Returns empty data.frame models match criteria.

title: List available models — list_models
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lms_server_start() lms_download("google/gemma-3-1b") lms_load("google/gemma-3-1b")  # List all downloaded models list_models()  # List only currently loaded models list_models(loaded = TRUE)  # Get detailed information about loaded text models list_models(loaded = TRUE, type = "llm", detailed = TRUE) } # }

what: Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat
title: Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat
text: Send prompt locally running LM Studio model. wrapper automatically routes request appropriate subfunction based selected API type.

title: Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat
code: lms_chat(   model,   input,   system_prompt = NULL,   host = "http://localhost:1234",   api_type = c("openresponses", "openai", "native"),   logprobs = FALSE,   simplify = TRUE,   ... )

title: Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat
text: model Character. name loaded model. input Character. user prompt send model. system_prompt Character. optional system prompt guide model behavior. host Character. base URL LM Studio server. Default "http://localhost:1234". api_type Character. LM Studio API endpoint use. Options "openresponses" (default), "openai", "native". logprobs Logical. Whether return log probabilities generated tokens. Default FALSE. simplify Logical. TRUE, extracts core text response. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed selected API body.

title: Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat
text: Depending arguments provided: simplify = FALSE, returns parsed list raw JSON response. simplify = TRUE logprobs = FALSE, returns single character string containing model's text response. simplify = TRUE logprobs = TRUE (chosen API type supports ), returns object class lms_chat_result containing text data.frame token probabilities.

what: Batch Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat_batch
title: Batch Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat_batch
text: Process vector inputs sequentially LM Studio.

title: Batch Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat_batch
code: lms_chat_batch(   model,   inputs,   system_prompt = NULL,   format = c("vector", "list", "data.frame"),   host = "http://localhost:1234",   simplify = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   ... )

title: Batch Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat_batch
text: model Character. loaded model name. inputs Character vector. prompts process. system_prompt Character. Optional system prompt. format Character. Output format: "vector", "list", "data.frame". host Character. Server URL. simplify Logical. TRUE, parses outputs. quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress bar. ... Additional arguments passed lms_chat.

title: Batch Chat Completion with LM Studio — lms_chat_batch
text: return type depends format argument: "vector": character vector responses. format supported simplify = TRUE logprobs = FALSE. "list": list element response corresponding provided input. "data.frame": data.frame containing input output columns. logprobs = TRUE, additional list-column named logprobs included.

what: Chat Completion via Native API — lms_chat_native
title: Chat Completion via Native API — lms_chat_native
text: Direct interface LM Studio's v1 Native endpoint. Optimized stateful chats hardware control.

title: Chat Completion via Native API — lms_chat_native
code: lms_chat_native(   model,   input,   system_prompt = NULL,   host = "http://localhost:1234",   simplify = TRUE,   ... )

title: Chat Completion via Native API — lms_chat_native
text: model Character. loaded model name. input Character. user prompt. system_prompt Character. Optional system prompt. host Character. Server URL. simplify Logical. TRUE, parses output text. ... Additional API arguments.

title: Chat Completion via Native API — lms_chat_native
text: simplify = FALSE, returns list representing raw JSON response. simplify = TRUE, returns character string containing model's text output.

what: Chat Completion via OpenAI Compatibility API — lms_chat_openai
title: Chat Completion via OpenAI Compatibility API — lms_chat_openai
text: Direct interface LM Studio's OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Uses messages array format.

title: Chat Completion via OpenAI Compatibility API — lms_chat_openai
code: lms_chat_openai(   model,   messages,   host = "http://localhost:1234",   logprobs = FALSE,   simplify = TRUE,   ... )

title: Chat Completion via OpenAI Compatibility API — lms_chat_openai
text: model Character. loaded model name. messages List. structured list role content pairs. host Character. Server URL. logprobs Logical. Whether request logprobs (currently stubbed LM Studio). simplify Logical. TRUE, parses output text. ... Additional API arguments.

title: Chat Completion via OpenAI Compatibility API — lms_chat_openai
text: simplify = FALSE, returns list representing raw JSON response. Otherwise, returns character string containing generated text. logprobs = TRUE, returns lms_chat_result object log probabilities populated NULL since currently stubbed LM Studio OpenAI endpoint.

what: Chat Completion via OpenResponses API — lms_chat_openresponses
title: Chat Completion via OpenResponses API — lms_chat_openresponses
text: Direct interface LM Studio's OpenResponses endpoint. Supports logprobs custom instructions.

title: Chat Completion via OpenResponses API — lms_chat_openresponses
code: lms_chat_openresponses(   model,   input,   instructions = NULL,   host = "http://localhost:1234",   logprobs = FALSE,   simplify = TRUE,   ... )

title: Chat Completion via OpenResponses API — lms_chat_openresponses
text: model Character. loaded model name. input Character. user prompt. instructions Character. Optional system instructions. host Character. Server URL. logprobs Logical. Whether return token probabilities. simplify Logical. TRUE, parses output text dataframe. FALSE, returns raw list. ... Additional API arguments (e.g., top_logprobs, temperature).

title: Chat Completion via OpenResponses API — lms_chat_openresponses
text: simplify = FALSE, returns list representing raw JSON response. Otherwise, returns character string containing generated text. logprobs = TRUE, returns object class lms_chat_result incorporating text probability data.

Quantization level model download (e.g., "Q4_K_M"). supported Hugging Face links. host Character. host address local server. Defaults "http://localhost:1234". ... Additional arguments passed request.

what: Load a model via REST API — lms_load
title: Load a model via REST API — lms_load
text: Load model via REST API

title: Load a model via REST API — lms_load
code: lms_load(   model,   context_length = NULL,   eval_batch_size = NULL,   flash_attention = NULL,   num_experts = NULL,   offload_kv_cache_to_gpu = NULL,   echo_load_config = FALSE,   force = FALSE,   host = "http://localhost:1234",   ... )

title: Load a model via REST API — lms_load
text: model Character. Unique identifier model load. context_length Integer. Maximum number tokens model consider. eval_batch_size Integer. Number input tokens process together single batch evaluation. flash_attention Logical. Whether optimize attention computation. num_experts Integer. Number experts use inference MoE models. offload_kv_cache_to_gpu Logical. Whether KV cache offloaded GPU memory. echo_load_config Logical. TRUE, echoes final load configuration response. force Logical. TRUE, bypasses check currently loaded models requests new instance server. Note overwrite replace existing model; loads second concurrent instance VRAM. Defaults FALSE. host Character. host address local server. Defaults "http://localhost:1234". ... Additional arguments passed API request body (useful future API parameters).

title: Load a model via REST API — lms_load
text: Invisibly returns character string loaded model identifier upon success. echo_load_config = TRUE, instead invisibly returns list containing model's detailed load configuration.

title: Load a model via REST API — lms_load
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lms_server_start() lms_download("google/gemma-3-1b")  # Load a model with default settings lms_load("google/gemma-3-1b")  # Load a model with custom context length and flash attention enabled lms_load("google/gemma-3-1b", context_length = 8192, flash_attention = TRUE) } # }

what: Calculate Expected Scores and Uncertainty from Logprobs — lms_score_expected
title: Calculate Expected Scores and Uncertainty from Logprobs — lms_score_expected
text: Takes logprobs dataframe (lms_chat_result) calculates weighted average score, normalized probabilities, uncertainty metrics.

title: Calculate Expected Scores and Uncertainty from Logprobs — lms_score_expected
code: lms_score_expected(lp_df, scale = 1:5)

title: Calculate Expected Scores and Uncertainty from Logprobs — lms_score_expected
text: lp_df dataframe logprobs (e.g., x$logprobs). scale Numeric vector. valid labels (e.g., 1:5).

title: Calculate Expected Scores and Uncertainty from Logprobs — lms_score_expected
text: named list containing three numeric elements (expected_value, weighted_sd, entropy) data.frame named probabilities columns label prob. Returns NULL input dataframe empty invalid.

title: Calculate Expected Scores and Uncertainty from Logprobs — lms_score_expected
code: # Create a sample logprobs dataframe representing a model's generation step mock_logprobs <- data.frame(   step_token = rep("4", 3),   step_logprob = rep(0, 3),   candidate_token = c("4", "5", "3"),   candidate_logprob = c(-0.105, -2.302, -3.506),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Calculate the expected score and uncertainty metrics lms_score_expected(mock_logprobs, scale = 1:5) #> $expected_value #> [1] 4.067975 #>  #> $weighted_sd #> [1] 0.3487363 #>  #> $entropy #> [1] 0.6454112 #>  #> $probabilities #>   label       prob #> 1     4 0.87376233 #> 2     5 0.09710651 #> 3     3 0.02913116 #>

code: lms_server_start(port = NULL, cors = FALSE)

text: port Integer. Port run server . provided, LM Studio uses last used port. cors Logical. Enable CORS support web application development. Defaults FALSE.

title: Start the LM Studio local server — lms_server_start
text: Invisibly returns integer representing system exit code (0 success).

title: Start the LM Studio local server — lms_server_start

what: Check the status of the LM Studio server — lms_server_status

text: Displays current status LM Studio local server via CLI, including whether running configuration.

title: Check the status of the LM Studio server — lms_server_status
code: lms_server_status(   json = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE,   log_level = NULL )

title: Check the status of the LM Studio server — lms_server_status
text: json Logical. Output status machine-readable JSON format. verbose Logical. Enable detailed logging output. quiet Logical. Suppress logging output. log_level Character. level logging use (e.g., "info", "debug").

title: Check the status of the LM Studio server — lms_server_status
text: default, returns character vector containing raw CLI output. json = TRUE jsonlite package available, returns parsed list data.frame status configuration.

title: Check the status of the LM Studio server — lms_server_status
text: can use one logging control flag time (verbose, quiet, log_level).

title: Check the status of the LM Studio server — lms_server_status

what: Unload a model from memory via REST API — lms_unload
title: Unload a model from memory via REST API — lms_unload
text: Unload model memory via REST API

title: Unload a model from memory via REST API — lms_unload
code: lms_unload(model, host = "http://localhost:1234", ...)

title: Unload a model from memory via REST API — lms_unload
text: model Character. Unique identifier (instance_id) model instance unload. host Character. host address local server. Defaults "http://localhost:1234". ... Additional arguments passed API request body.

title: Unload a model from memory via REST API — lms_unload
text: Invisibly returns character string representing unloaded instance_id upon success.

title: Unload a model from memory via REST API — lms_unload
text: loaded multiple instances model using force = TRUE lms_load(), server assigns unique instance identifiers (e.g., "google/gemma-3-1b" "google/gemma-3-1b:2"). Passing base model name lms_unload() unload primary instance. unload duplicate instances, must provide exact instance_id, use lms_unload_all() clear everything.

title: Unload a model from memory via REST API — lms_unload
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lms_server_start() lms_download("google/gemma-3-1b") lms_load("google/gemma-3-1b")  # Unload a single specific model lms_unload("google/gemma-3-1b") } # }

what: Unload all models from memory — lms_unload_all
title: Unload all models from memory — lms_unload_all
text: Retrieves list currently loaded models unloads one one.

title: Unload all models from memory — lms_unload_all
code: lms_unload_all(host = "http://localhost:1234", ...)

title: Unload all models from memory — lms_unload_all
text: host Character. host address local server. Defaults "http://localhost:1234". ... Additional arguments passed API request body unload request.

title: Unload all models from memory — lms_unload_all
text: Invisibly returns character vector instance_ids successfully unloaded. models currently loaded, invisibly returns NULL.

title: Unload all models from memory — lms_unload_all
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lms_server_start() lms_download("google/gemma-3-1b") lms_load("google/gemma-3-1b")  # Unload all currently loaded models to clear VRAM lms_unload_all() } # }

what: rlmstudio: Access and Control LM Studio — rlmstudio-package
title: rlmstudio: Access and Control LM Studio — rlmstudio-package
text: community-maintained 'R' wrapper 'LM Studio' command line interface API. Provides functions manage local daemon server, download load models, interact Large Language Models (LLMs).

text: Maintainer: Jeffrey Girard @jmgirard.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Jeffrey Girard @jmgirard.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Run code with the LM Studio daemon active — with_lms_daemon
title: Run code with the LM Studio daemon active — with_lms_daemon
text: Temporarily starts LM Studio headless daemon, executes provided R expression, gracefully shuts daemon active servers . ideal automated scripts pipelines.

title: Run code with the LM Studio daemon active — with_lms_daemon
code: with_lms_daemon(code)

title: Run code with the LM Studio daemon active — with_lms_daemon
text: code R expression execute daemon running.

title: Run code with the LM Studio daemon active — with_lms_daemon
text: result evaluated code.

title: Run code with the LM Studio daemon active — with_lms_daemon
text: cautious using wrapper already LM Studio GUI open. setup phase (lms_daemon_start) succeed, teardown phase (lms_daemon_stop) fail CLI prevents programmatic shutdowns graphical interface. wrapper best reserved strictly headless environments fully automated scripts.

title: Run code with the LM Studio daemon active — with_lms_daemon
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- with_lms_daemon({   lms_load("llama-3.1-8b")   lms_chat("llama-3.1-8b", input = "Hello world!") }) } # }
